story_3_13_engine_tasks_workflows.title: Engine/Tasks/Workflows

dependencies.key_insight: Use fs.readdirSync() with recursive option or walk-sync library for YAML discovery
dependencies.implementation_notes: Existing pattern in codebase - copy from packages/api/src/services/stories.ts

dependencies.pattern: HTTP endpoint health check with timeout
dependencies.key_insight: Use axios or node-fetch with timeout configuration. Graceful fallback on timeout/failure.
implementation_notes: Set timeout to 2000ms
Return engine_available: true/false flag
Never return fake data when engine unavailable
Use AbortController for request cancellation

dependencies.reference: Located in packages/api/src/services/ - use existing implementation
dependencies.key_insight: Call collectCurrentMetrics() and format response with health indicators
implementation_notes: Import SystemMetricsCollector from existing service
Map system metrics to EngineStatus interface
Calculate health based on CPU threshold (<90% = healthy)

critical_patterns: Error handling: never return fake data as fallback
Honest flags: engine_available boolean must reflect real state
Graceful degradation: UI must handle unavailable engine without crashing
blockers: Must verify SystemMetricsCollector exists and works
Must confirm engine health endpoint URL and response format

story_3_16_analytics_routes.title: Missing Analytics & Routes

dependencies.key_insight: All 12 agents documented in agent authority matrix: @dev, @qa, @architect, @pm, @po, @sm, @analyst, @data-engineer, @ux-design-expert, @devops, @aiox-master, etc.
implementation_notes: Response format: { id, name, icon, role, status }
Status can be: 'online', 'offline', 'idle'
Use hardcoded list for MVP - agents are static

dependencies.key_insight: Squads can be extracted from: (1) core-config.yaml squads section, (2) team_members table by squad_id grouping, (3) hard-coded project structure
implementation_notes: Check core-config.yaml first for static squad definition
Fall back to team_members table if exists
Response: { id, name, description, members_count, status }
Multiple data sources = need integration orchestration

dependencies.key_insight: MCP servers are documented in mcp-registry.json. Need to read and format response.
implementation_notes: Response format: { id, name, type, status, version }
Status: enabled, disabled, error
Use existing registry file as data source

dependencies.reference: Combine agent metrics, system metrics, squad productivity
dependencies.key_insight: Analytics endpoint needs to aggregate from: metrics table, workflows, stories, agent activity
implementation_notes: Create aggregation queries for: total_agents, active_agents, total_workflows, total_stories
Cache results or compute on-demand (query performance impact)
Response format: { overview: { ... }, timestamp }

dependencies.key_insight: Performance endpoint aggregates: success_rate, average_latency, error_count by agent
implementation_notes: Query metrics table with agent_id grouping
Calculate percentiles: p50, p95, p99 latency
Time window: last 24h, last 7d, last 30d options
Handle missing data gracefully

critical_patterns: Schema consistency: all endpoints must use consistent response format
Data source multiplicity: coordinate between hardcoded, file-based, and DB sources
Performance: aggregation queries may be slow - consider caching strategy
blockers: Must verify squad data source availability (core-config.yaml vs team_members table)
Must confirm MCP registry file location and format
Must validate metrics table schema for aggregation queries

story_3_17_complete_integration.title: Complete Data Integration

dependencies.reference: Batch persistence pattern - collect metrics and insert at interval
dependencies.key_insight: Don't insert on every metric update. Collect for 60s, then batch insert to Supabase.
implementation_notes: Use setInterval(60000) to trigger batch insert
Collect metrics in memory buffer
Insert to Supabase system_metrics table
Handle insert errors gracefully (log, don't crash)
Consider table cleanup: archive old records > 30 days

dependencies.key_insight: Table must support metrics collection: timestamp, agent_id, cpu, memory, latency, success_rate, etc.
implementation_notes: Columns: id (uuid), collected_at (timestamp), agent_id, cpu_percentage, memory_usage_mb, latency_ms, success_rate, error_count, processed_count
Index on: collected_at, agent_id for query performance
Retention: keep last 90 days, auto-archive older

dependencies.pattern: Remove DEMO flags and wire real data across 11 components
dependencies.key_insight: Large scope: touch many components. Risk of regression. Need careful planning.
implementation_notes: Components to refactor: AgentsMonitor, CostsMetrics, OverviewStats, RoadmapView, StoriesTab, SquadsTab, WorkflowsTab, EngineStatus, HandoffsTab, MemoryTab, AnalyticsTab
Strategy: one component at a time, test after each
Remove all DEMO conditional rendering and hardcoded data
Update all hooks: useAgents, useDashboard, useMetrics, etc.
Regression risk: need comprehensive testing

dependencies.pattern: Keep dashboard in sync with real data sources
dependencies.key_insight: Multiple data sources may update at different rates. Need strategy for consistency.
implementation_notes: Define refresh intervals for each data type: metrics (5s), stories (30s), workflows (30s), agents (10s)
Use React Query or SWR for cache management
Implement optimistic updates for user actions
Handle stale data gracefully (show 'data from 30s ago' indicator)

dependencies.pattern: Monitor impact of metrics collection on dashboard
dependencies.reference: Metrics collection runs every 60s. May impact UI responsiveness.
dependencies.key_insight: Metrics persistence must not block UI. Use async/background processing.
implementation_notes: Metrics collection should run in background (async, no await)
Monitor dashboard performance: FCP, LCP, CLS metrics
Implement error logging: if metrics insert fails, log but don't crash
Consider worker thread or background job queue for heavy operations

dependencies.pattern: Single source of truth for dashboard state
dependencies.reference: Current hook may be tightly coupled to mock data
dependencies.key_insight: Hook must aggregate data from multiple real API endpoints and manage loading/error states.
implementation_notes: Refactor hook to: (1) fetch from all endpoints in parallel, (2) handle loading/error for each, (3) merge results, (4) provide unified state
Use Promise.allSettled() for resilience (one endpoint failing shouldn't crash others)
Implement retry logic for transient failures
Cache results to avoid re-fetching on every render

critical_patterns: Async non-blocking metrics collection: background task, not blocking
Graceful degradation: if any data source fails, show what's available
Data honesty: never use fake data. Show empty state if no real data.
Performance first: metrics collection must not impact dashboard UX
integration_order: 3. useDashboard hook refactoring (aggregate all real endpoints)
4. Component refactoring one at a time: remove DEMO, wire real data
5. Real-time sync: implement polling intervals and cache strategy
6. Performance testing: ensure no regression from metrics collection
blockers: Must have Supabase team_members table from Phase 0
Must confirm SystemMetricsCollector exists and is working
Must validate all 5 Phase 2 endpoints exist before wiring components
Must test metrics collection performance impact before deploying

file_i_o_pattern.description: Multiple stories read files: YAML workflows, stories, handoffs, memory files
file_i_o_pattern.recommendation: Create shared utility: FileSystemReader with caching

supabase_integration.description: Multiple stories read/write to Supabase: team_members, metrics, etc.
supabase_integration.recommendation: Centralize Supabase queries. Use prepared statements to avoid N+1.

api_endpoint_schema.description: All endpoints must use consistent response format
api_endpoint_schema.pattern: { data: T[], total: number, limit?: number }
api_endpoint_schema.recommendation: Define ApiResponse<T> interface in shared types

error_handling.description: Consistent error handling across all new endpoints
error_handling.pattern: Never return fake data. Return { data: [], total: 0 } or error response
error_handling.recommendation: Create error handler middleware: standardize error format

research_summary.estimated_additional_effort: 8-12 hours for utility development and testing

notes: Research complete. All dependencies identified. Ready for spec writing. Recommend starting with Phase 0 (Supabase setup) as prerequisite before any story implementation.
